All users of coderef-pipeline CLI command (passive — backwards-compatible fix)

risks.title: Positional path fallback breaks existing --project-dir users
risks.mitigation: Positional arg is additive — --project-dir still works, positional is a new alias. If yargs/minimist is used, test precedence order explicitly.

risks.title: CLI entry point is compiled/generated — editing .js source doesn't take effect
risks.mitigation: Task 1.1 audit identifies exact source file and whether a build step is needed. If TypeScript source, edit .ts file and rebuild before testing.

candidates.role: primary — CLI entry point where --project-dir is parsed
reference doc — must reflect updated --help output

dependencies: yargs/minimist/commander — whichever arg parser coderef-pipeline uses (TBD via audit)
CODEREF-CORE build step — if TypeScript source, run tsc or npm run build after editing

decision_records.decision: Light plan type — no discovery needed
decision_records.rationale: Single-file CLI UX fix. No architectural change, no cross-layer impact. Light plan is appropriate.
The primary fix is the error message + --help improvement. Positional fallback is a nice-to-have that the implementer should judge based on how the CLI arg parser is set up.
